description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a white ceramic body and an attached rectangular cistern at the back. The toilet features a round seat and lid, both of which are raised, revealing a slightly stained bowl interior. The cistern has a visible flush lever on the left side, and the base is broad and flat, designed for stable floor mounting. The surface of the toilet is mostly smooth but shows signs of wear and discoloration, especially around the bowl and seat. The overall design is utilitarian, typical for public or residential restrooms, with no visible accessibility modifications. The object appears robust and heavy, suitable for fixed installation in urban facilities.
description: A white ceramic floor-mounted toilet with a raised seat and exposed cistern, showing some wear and discoloration. The flush lever is visible on the left side of the cistern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat, bowl interior, and the full face of the cistern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the flush lever and the profile of the seat and cistern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the base, with no visible functional parts.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the seat and cistern profile, with no flush lever visible.
